conjecture_title: Conjecture on unavoidable G-dependence in Theorem 2
paper_title: Extremal and Ramsey results on graph blowups
summary: Girão and Hancock (arXiv:2205.12826, European J. Combin. 120 (2024) 103984) proved the conjecture affirmatively: for every 3-chromatically connected graph H (including triangles), the dependence on G in the multiplicative factor a(G,H,r) of the bound B(G→rH;t) ≤ a·b^t is unavoidable. They also showed the complementary result that for forests F the factor a can be chosen independently of G.

since_posted.title: Two Ramsey problems in blowups of graphs
since_posted.claim: Proves the conjecture by establishing that for every 3-chromatically connected graph H (in particular the triangle K_3), the G-dependence in the multiplicative factor a(G,H,r) is necessary; conversely, for forests the factor a can be taken independent of G.

search_queries: Fox Luo Wigderson "graph blowups" Ramsey "multiplicative factor" "dependence on G" 1912.08328
graph blowup Ramsey "unavoidable" G-dependence exponential bound arXiv 2021 2022 2023 2024
notes: Conjecture settled affirmatively by Girão–Hancock arXiv:2205.12826 (European J. Combin. 2024): G-dependence in the prefactor is unavoidable for 3-chromatically connected H and unnecessary for forests.
